title: Boat Rental in St Vincent: A Practical Guide for 2026
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in St Vincent, where you typically depart from Kingstown or Blue Lagoon, means weighing cost, licence rules and itinerary against the Caribbean Sea conditions. Expect prices from EUR 150 for a half-day trip. BoatHire24 makes comparison straightforward.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in St Vincent?

Costs start at EUR 150 for a shared tour and rise to EUR 2,500 for a crewed yacht. The table below shows a quick price breakdown in EUR.

  • Shared group tour: from EUR 80 per person (half-day, up to 12 guests)
  • Small motorboat (6m, no skipper): EUR 150 for a half-day, EUR 250 for a full day
  • Catamaran (like a Lagoon 42, with skipper): EUR 800 for a full day (up to 12)
  • Crewed sailing yacht (Azimut 50): EUR 1,500, 2,500 for a full day

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in St Vincent and the Grenadines?

You need a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or an equivalent national licence. The operator will confirm the exact requirement at booking. Most bareboat charters insist on the ICC; skippered charters require none.

Booking a skippered charter avoids licence worries entirely and often reveals secret spots like the Tobago Cays that you might miss on your own.

Where to set off in St Vincent

The main gateway is Kingstown Harbour, which shelters the main ferry and yacht docks. Blue Lagoon, just south of Kingstown, is a popular anchorage for charter boats. If you want a quieter start, head to Calliaqua Bay or the nearby marina at Villa Point.

For trips north, consider departing from Chateaubelair Bay, closer to the La Soufrière volcano. For Grenadine voyages, start from Bequia, a short ferry hop from St Vincent, where Admiralty Bay offers a well-sheltered anchorage.

Which boat to rent in St Vincent

The type of boat you choose depends on your plans and how many guests you bring. For a broader selection, you can find a skippered charter easily on BoatHire24.

  1. Motorboat (open console), ideal for a fast day trip to Mustique (30 nm). Carries 4, 6 guests. From EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (Lagoon 42), great for families and larger groups wanting stable sailing. Up to 12 guests. From EUR 800 full day.
  3. Sailing yacht (Fairline 40), a classic choice for couples who enjoy hands-on sailing. 2, 4 guests. From EUR 400 half-day.
  4. Speedboat (Olympic 490 Rhodes), perfect for water-skiing or zipping between anchorages. Up to 5 guests. See this speedboat listing for current rates.
  5. Day cruiser (Axopar 28), a modern, versatile boat for coastal exploration. Up to 8 guests. From EUR 350 half-day.

When is the best season to rent a boat in St Vincent?

The high season runs from December to April, when the north-east trade winds are steady and the sea is calm. This is the ideal time for a bareboat or skippered charter. The low season (June to November) brings higher humidity and occasional squalls, but prices often drop by 20, 30 percent.

Local advice: the best months for a day trip to the Tobago Cays are January through April, when the wind is around 15 knots and visibility is excellent.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, 2,500 per day
  • Peak months: December, April
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4h) or full day (8h)
  • Max capacity: 2, 12 guests depending on vessel
  • Currency: EUR (most listings also accept USD)

Questions about boat rental in St Vincent

Can you rent a boat without a skipper in St Vincent?

Yes, bareboat charters are available for qualified sailors who hold an ICC or equivalent.

What is the best departure point for the Grenadines?

Kingstown Harbour is the main departure point, but many choose Bequia’s Admiralty Bay for a shorter crossing to Mustique or the Tobago Cays.

Are there any age restrictions for renting a boat?

Minimum age is typically 25 years old for bareboat charters; skippered trips have no set age limit for passengers.

What marine weather should you expect in summer 2026?

Summer sees light winds and higher humidity, with occasional thunderstorms; always check the forecast before heading out.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in St Vincent or list your own. For more options, browse the listings on boathire24.com and consider becoming a host if you own a vessel here.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in St Vincent and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
